title: Department lead lookup
prompt_md:
## Goal

Model an **aggregation**: a `Department` references a `Teacher` it does not own. The teacher is created independently of the department.

## Input

- Line 1: the teacher's name (a single word).
- Line 2: the department name (a single word).

## Output

A single line: `<department>: <teacher>`.

## Notes

- `Department` must hold a **reference (or non-owning pointer)** to a `Teacher` created outside it; it must not create or destroy the teacher.
- Create the teacher first so it outlives the department.

title: Shared teacher across departments
prompt_md:
## Goal

Show that one `Teacher` can be aggregated by several departments at once. Two departments share the same teacher; after both departments are destroyed, the teacher is still alive.

## Input

- Line 1: the teacher's name (a single word).
- Line 2: two department names separated by a space (single words).

## Output

```
<dept1> lead: <teacher>
<dept2> lead: <teacher>
<teacher> still exists
```

## Notes

- Both departments must reference the **same single** `Teacher` object (no copies).
- Destroy the departments before printing the final line, so it proves the teacher outlived them.

test_cases.label: Teacher shared across two departments
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Science lead: Garcia
Engineering lead: Garcia
Garcia still exists

test_cases.label: Different names
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Biology lead: Park
Chemistry lead: Park
Park still exists

test_cases.label: Short names
test_cases.expected_stdout:
AI lead: Wu
ML lead: Wu
Wu still exists
